dimensions.notes: changed_files lives on the work item; buildCompletion reads it. The reporter is the owner subagent via record-result.
Two candidates (handoff #1): --changed-files flag vs node reports. Flag only relocates the manual pin; node-report removes the friction.
Union is pass-only (failed partial edits reported on eventual pass). Dedup preserves existing order. No node-kind gate — work-item-level metadata any node may contribute.

questions.question: Where do changed_files accumulate so `autopilot complete` reads them automatically?
questions.why_matters: Determines the write target and which surface owns the field.
questions.answer: Union into work-item.changed_files inside recordResult on a contentful pass; complete already reads it.

questions.question: Should the operator pass changed_files at complete time, or should the mutating node report them as it runs?
questions.why_matters: Only the node-report path actually removes the per-run manual pin friction.
questions.answer: Node reports via record-result payload (approach a). The owner that changed files names them; they accumulate.

questions.question: On which outcomes does the union apply and how are duplicates handled?
questions.why_matters: Pass-only + dedup keeps the field a faithful, stable record of the run.
questions.answer: Pass-only; dedup against existing, append new in order. Not gated on node kind.

assumptions.statement: Union into work-item.changed_files inside recordResult on a contentful pass; complete already reads it.
assumptions.label: hypothesis

assumptions.statement: Node reports via record-result payload (approach a). The owner that changed files names them; they accumulate.
assumptions.label: hypothesis

assumptions.statement: Pass-only; dedup against existing, append new in order. Not gated on node kind.
assumptions.label: hypothesis
